calendar.matters:
Petition For Transfer Of Action And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,838.35; Memorandum In Support; And Declaration Of Gianna Gruenwald
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Deborah Fox for Nelson Jeong. Ciaran O'Sullivan for Lily Sabuni.

The Court stated that the underlying petition is off calendar and that we are left with the petition for sanctions. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that he is unclear why the petition is off calendar and asked if it could go back on. The Court stated that there is no present request to compel the trustee. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that his client attempted to meet and confer, that it was then determined that venue was incorrect, that their stipulation to change venue was rejected and they submitted the petition for sanctions, that they have not increased fees even though they have attended two hearings. Ms. Fox stated that there is no matter before the court to adjudicate. The Court stated that it retains jurisdiction over something that was brought in bad faith even though the underlying petition is off calendar. Ms. Fox cited CCP, that there is no prevailing party, that the trustor died in San Francisco, that all of the assets are in San Francisco, that after a year of no accountings it seemed that a professional was needed and that San Francisco was the proper venue, that the parties are mediating on November 18, 2015, that it is inconsistent to now say there is no venue, that 396(d) provides the court with authority to retain venue and that there is no prevailing party and no finding of bad faith. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that it is inconsistent to invoke 396(d) after the petition is off calendar and agreed that the award of sanctions is at the discretion of the court. The Court denied the petition to transfer of action and for sanctions, stated that there was sufficient basis when the petition was filed to believe that venue was correct, that the remaining petition is resolved and now off calendar, that nothing happened here and that there is nothing to transfer or act on.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order;(1) Compelling Trustee To Account;(2) To Remove Trustee Due To Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And Request Etc.
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Off Calendar

Petition For Transfer Of Action And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,838.35; Memorandum In Support; And Declaration Of Gianna Gruenwald
Off Calendar Sep-30-2015 Continued To Oct-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for (petitioner) Nelson Jeong. Ciaran O'Sullivan for (trustee) Lily Jeong Sabuni.

The Court stated that there are two petitions and that there are questions regarding venue. Mr. Der stated that he would like to continue the matter and is moving towards mediation. The Court stated that venue is clearly set out and that it cannot take this on just because you want it here. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that he had a recent case with the same issues, that the court allowed them to mediate and that they would like to do the same here. The Court stated it is different circumstances, that there is no venue in this jurisdiction for this case, that it encourages the parties to settle but cannot put this courts resources into this case. Mr. Der stated that they would like to continue to attempt to mediate. The Court suggested that they take the petitions off calendar, try to settle and then refile if necessary. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that a pro bono mediator has already agreed to a time and place and that they would be grateful for the referral. The Court stated that it is not comfortable referring this matter to mediation. Mr. Der suggested a continuance to discuss how to proceed. The court stated that it uses court resources to continue a matter, that it will continue the matter to 10/14/15 and expects the parties to either take the petitions off calendar or produce a basis as to why venue is proper. The petition for transfer of action and for sanctions in the amount of $3,838.35, etc., and the petition for order compelling trustee to account, etc., were continued to October 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputies Watts & Siu. (Not Reported)
